frameworks.name: auditory stimulus reconstruction
frameworks.one_line: decoding the heard acoustic stimulus from population neural activity

frameworks.name: deep neural network regression
frameworks.one_line: nonlinear DNN maps cortical activity directly to synthesizer parameters

frameworks.name: linear regression decoding
frameworks.one_line: baseline linear mapping from neural activity to auditory spectrogram

frameworks.name: speech brain-computer interface
frameworks.one_line: motivating goal of a speech neuroprosthesis for direct brain communication

core_question: Can combining deep learning with speech synthesis improve intelligibility of speech reconstructed from human auditory cortex?
key_assumptions: evoked cortical activity carries sufficient information to recover the acoustic stimulus
speech synthesizer parameters are a tractable target for neural decoding
nonlinear models can capture neural-to-acoustic mappings better than linear ones
manipulations: regression method (linear vs deep neural network)
acoustic target representation (auditory spectrogram vs speech synthesis parameters)
subset of neural frequency bands used as input
key_findings: DNN estimating synthesizer parameters from all neural frequencies achieves highest scores
intelligibility improves by 65% over the linear-spectrogram baseline
both subjective and objective digit-recognition scores favor the DNN-synthesizer model
interpretations: deep learning plus speech synthesis advances the state-of-the-art in speech neuroprosthesis
closed-set intelligible speech reconstruction from auditory cortex is feasible
limitations_noted: evaluation limited to closed-set digit recognition
